- Verify all unique RNCs (dedup)
- Smart folder naming from extracted date/provider
- Consolidated multi-receipt markdown reports
- Summary with combined totals/ITBIS

validateNCF():

- Proper format validation (11 chars B-series, 13 chars E-series)

generateReceiptMarkdown():
- Line items table with ITBIS indicators
- DGII verification status and registered name
- Confidence score display
- Multi-receipt consolidated report format

fix(#216/#219): GPU alignment guard, Quick Actions tab, Resident Agent architecture (#221)

* fix(#216): GPU alignment guard, disable FunctionGemma concurrent load

- Add safeTokenCount() to avoid powers-of-2 (4096, 8192) which trigger
  LiteRT GPU reshape::Eval alignment bug on Adreno GPUs
- Disable FunctionGemma eager loading — 289MB on background thread raced
  with E4B GPU init causing OOM (lmkd kill at oom_score_adj 0)
- Add InferenceLoadingService foreground service for GPU init OOM protection
- Add GPU kernel cacheDir for serialized OpenCL compilation
- Change slider to 500-step increments (avoids power-of-2 snap points)
- Update all defaults to non-power-of-2 values (4000, 2000, 1000, 8000)
- Gate E2B/E4B model selection behind download state in settings
- Set E2B/E4B isGated=false (HuggingFace ungated since Apr 2026)
- Restore eager model loading with loading screen

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(#216): quick actions tab with lazy FunctionGemma loading

- Add bottom navigation bar (Chats / Actions tabs)
- New ActionsScreen with FAB, bottom sheet input, action history
- New ActionsViewModel with lazy FunctionGemma init on first use
- Room: QuickActionEntity + QuickActionDao + migration v7→v8
- Bottom nav only shows on list/actions screens, not chat/settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#219): E4B-first sequential loading + Resident Agent architecture

- ChatViewModel: load E4B on GPU before FunctionGemma — full memory
  headroom during GPU kernel compilation peak (~4-5GB transient)
- ChatViewModel/initEngineWhenReady: release EmbeddingGemma only before
  E4B init; FG no longer released (E4B-first makes it safe)
- ActionsViewModel: remove unused InferenceEngine injection
- KernelNavHost: scope innerPadding to list/actions routes only — fixes
  phantom bottom padding below chat input
- specification.md: update hardware tiering table, token window note,
  skills section and roadmap to reflect Resident Agent Architecture
  (deprecate FunctionGemma, QuickIntentRouter Tier 2, E4B tool calling
  Tier 3 — see issues #218 #219 #220)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#221): address code review — mutex double-init, FG idempotency, location permission guard

- Wrap initEngineWhenReady() in gemma4InitMutex to prevent concurrent
  sendMessage() from racing during the ~20s GPU init window. Eliminates
  the double-close of EmbeddingGemma and potential 3.4GB GPU engine orphan.
- Add initMutex to FunctionGemmaRouter.initialize() so concurrent calls
  from ActionsViewModel and ChatViewModel serialize safely and are idempotent.
- Guard location permission request in MainActivity with checkSelfPermission —
  prevents the dialog firing on every onCreate() (rotation, process restart).

commit.message:
Implementar base de datos Room para caché persistente offline del mapa y la red de calles (#13)

* Add RoadNetworkCache and VM integration

Introduce a persistent RoadNetworkCache (disk-based, TTL, LRU per ~2km cells) and integrate it into WorldMapViewModel via a Context-aware Factory. Configure osmdroid caching and parameters in MainActivity and use the ViewModel.Factory when creating the view model. Improve OverpassRepository: expand fetch radius to 2000m, increase read timeout, better HTTP error handling, URLEncoding, and parsing optimizations. Update WorldMapViewModel to prefer cache on startup, save downloads to cache, add refetch cooldown/thresholds, and restructure the game loop. UI refinements in WorldMapScreen: use the Factory for the ViewModel, marker caching, smoother zoom animations, WebView JS calls formatting, layout/style polish, and loading/zoom behavior. Also add zoom constants in WorldMapState.

* Migrate road cache to Room; add tile cache

Replace the old file/JSON road-network cache with a Room-backed implementation and add a new per-provider tile cache.

- RoadNetworkCache: switched from file-based JSON to Room (uses RoadZone/RoadWay/RoadNode entities and RoadNetworkDao). Implements TTL checks, LRU eviction via DAO, atomic insertZoneWithData transaction, and reconstitution of MapWay from DB rows.
- Added PowDatabase (Room) created under filesDir so DB is persistent and excluded from external cleaners.
- Added TileCache: SQLite-backed tile storage per provider with TTL and simple LRU eviction, stored under filesDir as provider.mbtiles.
- Added Room entities (RoadEntities.kt) and DAO (RoadNetworkDao.kt) to support the new cache.
- MainMenuScreen: UI overhaul (layout tweaks, settings panel, provider selector) and now passes chosen MapProvider when navigating to the map.
- MainActivity: OSMDroid configuration simplified (uses app filesDir, explicit cache params), navigation transitions cleaned, and world map provider is applied to WorldMapViewModel; minor permission/location call formatting cleaned up.
- build.gradle.kts: added ksp plugin and Room dependencies (runtime/ktx + ksp compiler), reorganized some dependencies and simplified compileSdk setting.

These changes centralize persistent map data in Room/SQLite for reliability and atomicity, add a robust tile cache, and let users choose map providers via the main menu.

* Fix review issues: composite PK, TileCache context, SHA-256 URL key, CacheStatusWidget, DAO location, .gitignore

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gabrielhuav/PolitecnicoOpenWorld/sessions/89d7cab6-4830-4f6b-a113-948e9ad1a9c4

Co-authored-by: gabrielhuav <71236850+gabrielhuav@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use application context in TileCache

Store the application context (appContext) in TileCache instead of a generic Context to avoid leaking activity contexts. Updated tilesDir initialization and the TileDatabase SQLiteOpenHelper to use appContext/applicationContext and made the constructor parameter private.

* Migrate tile cache to Room (MapTileEntity/DAO)

Replace the old file/SQLite-based TileCache with a Room-backed implementation: add MapTileEntity and MapTileDao, refactor TileCache to use MapTileDao (insert/get/count/deleteOldest), and remove the custom SQLiteOpenHelper logic. Improve CachingWebViewClient to normalize tile URLs, hash the normalized URL for cache keys, only intercept GET requests, refine MIME guessing and logging, and download using the original URL. Wire the new DAO into PowDatabase (add mapTileDao, include MapTileEntity) and bump DB version to 3; update WorldMapViewModel to obtain the mapTileDao and pass it into TileCache. Includes LRU eviction via DAO when MAX_TILES_PER_PROVIDER is exceeded.

* Add cache & FPS widget toggles and sync UI

Introduce showCacheWidget and showFpsWidget state and controls across the main menu and world map. MainMenuState and WorldMapState gain new flags, MainMenuViewModel exposes toggleCacheWidget/toggleFpsWidget and MainMenu UI now lets users enable/disable cache and FPS widgets before starting. MainActivity navigation callback updated to pass cache/fps choices to WorldMapViewModel, and WorldMapViewModel adds update/toggle functions to apply them. WorldMapScreen now computes real-time FPS (when enabled) and shows/hides diagnostic widgets (cache status and FPS) with animated visibility; also contains small UI/formatting cleanups. Function signatures and some composables were adjusted to wire the new options end-to-end.

* Fix composite PK for tiles, empty cache guard, encoding fix, remove redundant withContext

docs: add comprehensive PR6 implementation plan (#32)

* docs: add comprehensive PR6 implementation plan

- Created pr6-import-job-plan.md with detailed specification
  * Worker architecture with PostgreSQL-backed job queue
  * File storage strategy (shared volume + object storage later)
  * 6-stage import pipeline (validate, parse, normalize, search, match, review)
  * Heartbeat-based job leasing with stale recovery
  * API endpoints for job CRUD and control (pause/resume/cancel)
  * Worker loop implementation with graceful shutdown
  * Stage runner stubs for simulated batch processing
  * UI components for job list and detail views
  * Testing strategy and acceptance criteria
  * 9 files total (under 10-file limit)

- Updated implementation-plan.md
  * Marked PR5 (dump ingestion) as deferred until dump access available
  * Linked PR6 to detailed implementation plan

- Updated README.md
  * Added PR6 as next in progress
  * Noted PR5 deferral
  * Linked to pr6-import-job-plan.md

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* refactor: move worker to apps/worker/ as separate process

- Changed worker from apps/api/src/api/worker.py to apps/worker/main.py
- Added apps/worker/__init__.py to make it a proper Python package
- Worker runs via 'python -m worker.main' instead of 'python -m api.worker'
- Worker imports from api package for shared models/services
- Updated file count to 10 (at limit, but necessary for proper separation)
- Worker is now truly independent process with its own directory
- Deployment: separate Docker container, horizontally scalable

This is cleaner separation of concerns - worker is not part of the API,
it's a separate service that happens to share database/models with API.

* refactor: move stage execution logic to worker

**Cleaner separation of responsibilities:**

API (apps/api/) owns:
- HTTP endpoints and routes
- Job state management (claim, heartbeat, transitions)
- Database CRUD operations
- Helper functions (get_current_stage, update_stage_progress)

Worker (apps/worker/) owns:
- Job execution loop
- Batch processing orchestration
- Stage runners (actual work: validate, parse, normalize, search, match, review)

**Changes:**
- Removed run_stage_batch() from API (was mixing concerns)
- Moved stage_runners.py from API to worker
- API now provides state management primitives only
- Worker calls API for state, runs its own stage logic
- Worker imports: claim_next_job, get_current_stage, update_stage_progress, etc.
- Worker has: STAGE_RUNNERS dict with all execution logic

**File count:** Still 10 (at limit)
- 4 backend API (down from 5)
- 3 worker (up from 2)
- 2 frontend
- 1 test

This is the right architecture: API shouldn't know how to execute jobs,
it should only manage their state. Worker owns execution.

* docs: complete eng review for PR6 with performance optimizations

- Addressed 8 performance issues (N+1 queries, file I/O, indexes, polling, etc.)
- Added composite index on (status, created_at) for efficient job claiming
- Implemented exponential backoff polling (2s→5s→10s) to reduce API load
- Updated worker to be FastAPI app with /health endpoints for K8s probes
- Moved worker health check from TODO to base scope (production essential)
- Added 8 TODOs for future enhancements (metrics, retry, priority, ETA, etc.)
- Documented 3 critical gaps (1 fixed: FileNotFoundError handler, 2 accepted)
- Updated heartbeat interval to 60s (halved write volume)
- Added performance optimizations section to plan
- Minor whitespace cleanup in CLAUDE.md

All issues resolved. Plan ready for implementation.

commit.message:
Task 27: Armor sprite progression — level-based tint overlay

Add per-draw tint color support to the sprite rendering pipeline so the
player character visually reflects their armor tier (levels 1–5).

Renderer changes:
- drawSprite() gains optional tintR/tintG/tintB parameters (default white)
- Textured pipeline push constants extended from 68 to 80 bytes
  (mat4 projection + int hasNormalMap + 3× float tint RGB)
- textured.frag multiplies sampled texel by the tint color

AnimationComponent changes:
- New m_tintR/G/B members and setTintColor(r,g,b) setter
- render() forwards tint to drawSprite()

Game integration (main.cpp):
- getArmorTint() lambda maps level 1–5 to color:
    1: white, 2: bronze, 3: silver, 4: gold, 5: platinum
- Tint applied in setupPlayer() (covers new-game and load-game)
- Tint updated on level-up in the dead-enemy XP-award loop

Docs: architecture.md updated, task moved to CompletedTasks.md.

commit.message:
Restructure: purpose-built agents with proper trigger routing

New agents:
  deployment-validator — triggered by Release pipeline (BuildSucceeded)
    Proactively checks health after every deploy, identifies which
    microservice was deployed, picks the right diagnostic skill.
  vm-ops-agent — triggered by Azure Monitor alerts (title contains 'disk')
    Handles VM infrastructure issues using disk-pressure-diagnosis skill.

Release triggers:
  Post-Deploy Validation → Release pipeline succeeds → deployment-validator
  Build Failure Investigation → Build pipeline fails → incident-handler

Incident filters:
  vm-disk-alert → title contains 'disk' → vm-ops-agent
  auto-investigate → all other Azure Monitor alerts → incident-handler

Release pipeline updated with 'services' parameter so agent can
read which microservices were deployed via GetPipelineRunVariables.

commit.message:
Fix Windows CI build failures by making spawn tests Unix-only

The tests for SpawnTool path resolution use 'ls' and absolute paths like
'/bin/bash' which are POSIX-specific and don't exist on Windows. These
tests were failing on the Windows CI runner.

Fixed by adding #[cfg(unix)] to both:
- test_spawn_validation
- test_spawn_path_resolution

This allows these tests to be skipped on Windows while still running on
Unix-like systems (Linux, macOS). All 82 tests pass locally.

feat(ui): add CardCarousel with Apple Wallet horizontal scroll animation

Sprint 24: Replace CardSwitcher tab bar + Hero Card with unified
CardCarousel component using CSS scroll-snap for native-feel swipe.

New component (CardCarousel.tsx - 647 lines):
- CSS scroll-snap-type: x mandatory for 60fps native physics
- IntersectionObserver-based active card detection
- Apple Wallet style: active scale(1.0), inactive scale(0.88)/opacity 0.55
- Desktop arrow navigation (44px buttons, hidden on mobile)
- Dot indicators (pills ≤7 cards, N/M text for 8+)
- Card face: gradient bg, chip, network badge, name, last-4, benefit count
- Luminance-based text color for readability on any gradient

Accessibility:
- ARIA tablist/tab pattern with roving tabIndex
- aria-live polite announcements on card change
- Full prefers-reduced-motion support (cards, arrows, dots)
- Keyboard: Arrow/Home/End navigation via scrollToIndex delegation

Design tokens (14 new CSS custom properties):
- Card dimensions, scale, opacity, shadows, transitions, dots
- All tokens in :root, @media dark, and .dark blocks
- --carousel-arrow-opacity token for arrow buttons

Dashboard integration:
- Replaces CardSwitcher import with CardCarousel
- Removes Hero Card IIFE section
- Benefits update after swipe completes (debounced 150ms)
- CardSwitcher.tsx retained as fallback (not deleted)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Task 26: Add XP bar and level HUD

Render an XP bar and level indicator in screen-space UI after
endFrame()/resetView(). The bar shows at bottom-left (20, windowH-40),
200x10px with dark gray background + black outline. Fill is gold when
leveling, cyan at max level (5). Level text 'Lv N' rendered above the
bar in white at font size 18.

HUD renders in both the main render path and the transition-active
render path so it stays visible during room transitions.

commit.message:
Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change (#16087)

* Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change

@playwright/cli 0.1.7 changed their git tag naming convention from
'v0.1.7' to '0.1.7' (dropped the 'v' prefix). This caused our
provenance verification to fail with WorkflowRefMismatch.

- Accept both tag formats (with and without 'v' prefix) in the
  validateWorkflowRef callback for forward/backward compatibility
- Add test verifying both tag formats pass validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add E2E test for aspire new with agent init and Playwright install

Exercises the full aspire new → agent init → Playwright CLI install
flow end-to-end, verifying no provenance verification errors appear.
This is the primary regression test for tag format changes in upstream
@playwright/cli releases.

The test accepts the agent init prompt (instead of declining), selects
Playwright CLI during skill selection, and fails fast if a provenance
verification error is detected during npm package installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-reset sort order when switching master set types (#61)

Sort options are conditional per mode (set-order only for official-set,
evolution-chain/grouped-by-pokemon only for pokemon-collection). If
a user switches modes while an invalid sort is selected, the sort
persists in localStorage but is no longer in the dropdown options.

Now resets to 'chronological' when the current sort is invalid for
the new master set type.

commit.message:
feat: model version management — admin assigns versions, Jetson auto-builds engines

Dashboard (DevicesPage):
- Fetch active model versions per leaf_type from model_registry
- Model version selector per leaf_type in device edit form
- model_versions included in desired_config on save
- Display model versions + engine status (ready/building/error) in device table
- Config sync check ignores Jetson-added fields (engine_status, applied_model_versions)

Jetson (sync_engine):
- Detect model_versions change in polled desired_config
- Auto-build: check engine cache → download ONNX → trtexec --fp16 → hot-swap
- Exact version ONNX download (REST query by leaf_type+version with RPC fallback)
- Upload built engines to Storage cache for reuse by other devices
- Report engine_status + applied_model_versions in reported_config
- Re-report config on build completion/failure so dashboard updates
- Thread-safe config persistence (_config_lock protects read-modify-write)
- get_model_version() API for correct version tracking in predictions

Jetson (inference):
- InferenceWorkerPool.hot_swap() — queue-based swap on CUDA worker thread
- _do_hot_swap: load new engine BEFORE destroying old (graceful degradation)
- 120s timeout for slow Jetson devices

Docs:
- jetson_deployment_guide: section 9.5 Model Version Management
- admin_dashboard_manual: updated Device Fleet Tab
- project_context: model version management architecture

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
